articles.title: IMD issues orange heatwave alert for eastern Telangana as monsoon advances - The New Indian Express
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heatwave conditions in eastern districts of Telangana over the next two days, while light to moderate rainfall is expected across the rest of the state. This contrasting weather pattern comes amid the gradual advancement of the southwest monsoon over Telangana.
According to the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, the northern limit of the southwest monsoon currently passes through Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, and Bhadradri Kothagudem, extending further eastward. Conditions remain favourable for the monsoon to advance into the remaining parts of Telangana and adjoining regions over the next four to five days.
The weather office noted that a trough extending from the west-central Bay of Bengal off the South Coastal Andhra Pradesh coast to south Tamil Nadu is influencing regional weather conditions. Meanwhile, a cyclonic circulation over south Telangana has weakened.
Despite the expected rainfall, maximum temperatures across Telangana will remain largely unchanged over the next three days. Eastern districts are likely to experience heatwave conditions, prompting the IMD to advise caution.
The department forecast light to moderate rain at isolated places in several districts on Monday and Tuesday. Heavy rainfall was already recorded in parts of Telangana during the last 24 hours, with Sangareddy district receiving 12 cm of rain. Several other areas also witnessed scattered showers.

articles.title: Rain Likely Across Telangana Even as Eastern Districts Remain Hot - Hyderabad Mail
articles.full_text:
HYDERABAD: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an orange alert for heatwave conditions in eastern districts of Telangana for the next two days. At the same time, several other parts of the state are likely to receive light to moderate rainfall.
The contrasting weather comes as the southwest monsoon gradually advances across Telangana.
According to the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, the northern limit of the monsoon currently passes through Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad and Bhadradri Kothagudem before extending eastward. Officials said weather conditions favour further monsoon progress into the remaining parts of Telangana and nearby regions over the next four to five days.
A trough stretching from the west-central Bay of Bengal off the south Andhra Pradesh coast to south Tamil Nadu is affecting weather conditions in the region. Meanwhile, the cyclonic circulation over south Telangana has weakened.
Despite the rainfall forecast, maximum temperatures are likely to remain almost unchanged for the next three days. Eastern districts may continue to experience heatwave conditions. The IMD has advised people to take precautions against extreme heat.
The department has forecast light to moderate rain at isolated places in several districts on Monday and Tuesday.
Heavy rainfall was recorded in parts of Telangana during the last 24 hours. Sangareddy district received 12 cm of rain. Several other areas also reported scattered showers.
This mix of heatwave conditions and rainfall highlights the changing weather pattern as the monsoon moves further into the state.
